description: eta_theory vs eta_empirical for L(chi4 mod 5) at (T0=46.13, sigma=1, J=10, H=1582 zeros)

parameters.note: Used 1582 L(chi4 mod 5) zeros instead of H=2500 due to mp.dirichlet evaluation cost. Sufficient since the Hermite-Gauss test function (centered at T0=46.13, sigma=1) is exponentially negligible at gamma >> T0; contribution of zeros above ~80 is < 1e-300.

verdict: Theory bound holds (eta_emp < eta_theory for all X) and tracks the same decay law: Pearson r=0.9935 in log-log, slope=1.12. The theoretical bound derived solely from the Hermite-Gauss basis and PNT generalizes from zeta to L(chi4 mod 5), confirming the noise floor is a basis/PNT property, not an arithmetic accident of zeta.
